name: Steel Mill Slab Design - CR2
content: Each produced slab must be filled to at least a specified minimum percentage of its selected capacity. For example, if the minimum fill percentage is 80, then every used slab must carry at least 80% of the slab size chosen for it.
value_info: `orders`: List of dicts, each with `size` (int) and `color` (int), representing customer orders.
`min_fill_percent`: Integer minimum fill percentage required for every used slab.
`ref_opt_val`: Optional integer reference optimum for total loss; null until filled in.

var1.descr: `order_to_slab`: List[int] of length n_orders, where order_to_slab[i] is the slab index assigned to order i.

var2.descr: `slab_loads`: List[int] of length n_slabs, where slab_loads[j] is the total weight of orders assigned to slab j.

var3.descr: `slab_losses`: List[int] of length n_slabs, where slab_losses[j] is the wasted capacity on slab j.

var4.descr: `total_loss`: Integer total wasted capacity across all slabs.

complexity.difficulty_rationale: Adds one localized utilization constraint per slab over the existing load and loss variables, with no new decision-variable families and no objective change.

cr_impact.notes: Adds one minimum-fill constraint for each slab using the existing ld and ls variables.
